clusters.title: Wild footage shows allegedly drunk driver unleashing chaos in carpark
clusters.neutral_headline: Footage shows allegedly drunk driver unleashing chaos in carpark

items.title: Wild footage shows allegedly drunk driver unleashing chaos in carpark
items.standfirst: A 62-year old woman was arrested in a toilet after allegedly crashing into five cars in a Sydney car park while over the drink-driving limit.
items.content: A 62-year-old woman has been arrested in a toilet after allegedly crashing into five cars in a Sydney car park while more than five times over the drink-driving limit.Footage shows an SUV veering into multiple cars in a supermarket car park in Turramurra, on the city's upper north shore. The driver, a 62-year-old woman, was found and arrested in a nearby toilet, police say.READ MORE: Iran mocks Australia after more footballers U-turn on asylum decision She allegedly gave a positive breath test.Secondary testing at Hornsby Police Station returned an alleged reading of 0.268, according to officers.She was issued a court attendance notice for the offences of high-range PCA and negligent driving. Her licence was also suspended.She will appear before court at a later date.READ MORE: Aussies to suffer fresh cash blow with inflation to surge to new highsDownload the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter hereNEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.

items.content: A 62-year old woman was arrested in a toilet after allegedly crashing into five cars in a Sydney car park while over the drink-driving limit.

items.title: Queensland arts minister ignored recommendation that new theatre be named after poet Oodgeroo Noonuccal
items.standfirst: Exclusive: Queensland Performing Arts Centre board nominated Oodgeroo as preferred name in 2024 but it was not one of four options put to public vote by LNPFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA Queensland government minister intervened to ensure that a new theatre would not be named after the Aboriginal poet Oodgeroo Noonuccal, overriding the theatre’s board, according to documents obtained under right to information laws.The late artist’s name is also set to be stripped from a state electorate, in draft electoral boundaries released by the state’s redistribution commission this week. The Liberal National party lobbied for the change. Continue reading...
items.content: Exclusive: Queensland Performing Arts Centre board nominated Oodgeroo as preferred name in 2024 but it was not one of four options put to public vote by LNPFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA Queensland government minister intervened to ensure that a new theatre would not be named after the Aboriginal poet Oodgeroo Noonuccal, overriding the theatre’s board, according to documents obtained under right to information laws.The late artist’s name is also set to be stripped from a state electorate, in draft electoral boundaries released by the state’s redistribution commission this week. The Liberal National party lobbied for the change. Continue reading...

items.title: No recession but inflation hike and increased cost-of-living pressure on the way, Jim Chalmers says
items.standfirst: Economists predict RBA will raise interest rates this week and in May – days before treasurer unveils budgetFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastHouseholds can expect significant additional cost-of-living pressures because of the war in the Middle East, with Jim Chalmers confirming that the government expects inflation to rise beyond 4.5% in Australia.But the treasurer said he did not expect the economy to fall into recession because of the war sparked by US and Israeli bombings in Iran. Continue reading...
items.content: Economists predict RBA will raise interest rates this week and in May – days before treasurer unveils budgetFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastHouseholds can expect significant additional cost-of-living pressures because of the war in the Middle East, with Jim Chalmers confirming that the government expects inflation to rise beyond 4.5% in Australia.But the treasurer said he did not expect the economy to fall into recession because of the war sparked by US and Israeli bombings in Iran. Continue reading...

items.title: NSW to crack down on property underquoting, forcing sellers to publish price guides on all listings
items.standfirst: Underquoting and ‘dummy bidding’ will attract fines of $110,000 in bid to create a ‘fair property market’Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe New South Wales government will introduce new laws this week to force property sellers to publish a price guide on all advertising, and impose a fivefold increase to fines for underquoting real estate agents.The government says the draft laws, first flagged last year, are aimed at cracking down on agents providing misleading price estimates on property listings, a practice often used to inflate interest. Continue reading...
items.content: Underquoting and ‘dummy bidding’ will attract fines of $110,000 in bid to create a ‘fair property market’Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe New South Wales government will introduce new laws this week to force property sellers to publish a price guide on all advertising, and impose a fivefold increase to fines for underquoting real estate agents.The government says the draft laws, first flagged last year, are aimed at cracking down on agents providing misleading price estimates on property listings, a practice often used to inflate interest. Continue reading...

items.title: Danish royals Frederik and Mary begin six-day Australia tour with visit to rain-soaked red centre
items.standfirst: Royal trip includes stops in Canberra, Melbourne and Hobart and aims to deepen trade tiesFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastDenmark’s King Frederik and Queen Mary have ended the first leg of their Australian visit with a sunrise trek to a famous Uluru watering hole.The royal couple walked to Muṯitjulu waterhole in Uluṟu-Kata Tjuṯa national park with traditional owners on Sunday morning. The culturally significant site is one of the few permanent water sources around the sandstone monolith and a regular attraction for visiting dignitaries. Continue reading...

items.title: After nearly three years, the robodebt report’s secret chapter has been unsealed. What does it reveal?
items.standfirst: Sealed section names officials who drafted unlawful debt recovery scheme – and who were referred to corruption watchdog for further investigationGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastFor almost three years, 56 pages of the robodebt royal commission’s final report were kept secret as investigating bodies considered whether charges or further findings could be made.The sealed chapter, as it would become known, was finally released to the Australian public this week after the National Anti-Corruption Commission (Nacc) delivered its report into six people referred for potential corruption. Continue reading...
items.content: Sealed section names officials who drafted unlawful debt recovery scheme – and who were referred to corruption watchdog for further investigationGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastFor almost three years, 56 pages of the robodebt royal commission’s final report were kept secret as investigating bodies considered whether charges or further findings could be made.The sealed chapter, as it would become known, was finally released to the Australian public this week after the National Anti-Corruption Commission (Nacc) delivered its report into six people referred for potential corruption. Continue reading...
